report_name: Accenture Bets $4.175B on OT Security, Acquiring Dragos, runZero, and NetRise

identities.name: Accenture
identities.description: The acquiring company, making a $4.175 billion strategic investment to build a dominant force in the operational technology (OT) and critical infrastructure cybersecurity market.

identities.name: Dragos
identities.description: An OT threat detection and response vendor in which Accenture acquired a majority stake. It will continue to operate as an independent business unit led by its current CEO, Robert M. Lee.

identities.name: runZero
identities.description: An asset intelligence and attack surface management firm fully acquired by Accenture. It will be integrated under the Dragos brand.

identities.name: NetRise
identities.description: A specialist in firmware and software supply chain security for extended IoT (xIoT) devices, fully acquired by Accenture. It will be integrated under the Dragos brand.

identities.name: Robert M. Lee
identities.description: CEO of Dragos, who will continue to lead the company following Accenture's acquisition of a majority stake.

identities.name: Critical Infrastructure
identities.description: The target customer sectors for the combined security platform offered by Accenture, Dragos, runZero, and NetRise.
